seoTitle: Lonesome Creek vs Long Flat — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Lonesome Creek and Long Flat. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Lonesome Creek vs Long Flat: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lonesome Creek and Long Flat.
heading: Lonesome Creek vs Long Flat — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lonesome Creek, Queensland and Long Flat, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lonesome Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Long Flat (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lonesome Creek or Long Flat safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lonesome Creek has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Long Flat scores 45/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lonesome Creek scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Long Flat scores 35/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lonesome Creek or Long Flat?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lonesome Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
